config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that opens the door to abuse by governments and majorities. In my view, a healthy society protects personal freedoms while limiting them only when there is a clear and necessary public interest. From my perspective, the best balance is to defend individual rights first, then restrict them only in narrow, justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman nilai-nilai di Amerika Serikat, justru hak individu adalah pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh negara maupun mayoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau dijadikan alasan default untuk mengalahkan kebebasan pribadi, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menang melawan individu, melainkan yang bisa membuktikan bahwa pembatasan hak benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence dissent, justify censorship, or pressure minorities. Society does matter, but it should not automatically outrank the individual, because once that principle is accepted, the weakest people are usually the first to lose protection. A good society is not one that always wins against the person; it is one that can prove a restriction is necessary, limited, and fair.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama terhadap kekuasaan masyarakat, negara, dan mayoritas. Dari perspektif Amerika Serikat, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika alasan “kepentingan sosial” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau pembatasan yang akhirnya merugikan kelompok kecil. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan kebebasan pribadi; pembatasan hanya sah kalau benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan proporsional. Kalau tidak, “demi masyarakat” mudah berubah menjadi dalih untuk menindas orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia, “society first” is too often used as a shortcut to silence minorities, restrict speech, or excuse unfair treatment. I do believe public interests matter, especially for safety and order, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights because that gives too much power to the majority and the state. A society is healthier when it can justify any restriction as necessary, narrow, and fair, not when it assumes the individual must always yield. If we weaken individual rights in the name of the common good, the people who suffer first are usually the least powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, bukan korban pertama, ketika masyarakat atau negara mengklaim bertindak demi kebaikan bersama. Dari pengalaman nilai-nilai di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “kepentingan sosial” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan itu justru paling berbahaya bagi kelompok yang rentan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hanya sah jika pembatasan benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan proporsional. Kalau tidak, masyarakat “diutamakan” sering kali hanya berarti mayoritas diberi kuasa untuk menekan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
